index.title: 3D Slicer for Latin America
index.imageCaption: 2025 Slicer Toluca Workshop, Facultad de Ingeniería, Universidad Autónoma del Estado de México
index.heroParagraph: Welcome to the 3D Slicer for Latin America initiative. Our mission is to bridge the gap between advanced medical imaging technology and the regional community through three core objectives. We are localizing the 3D Slicer platform into Latin America Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese to enhance accessibility. Simultaneously, we are engineering a new Slicer extension to automate the localization, testing, and versioning of tutorial workflows. Beyond technical development, we are organizing hands-on Slicer training workshops and outreach events using localized version of 3D Slicer to empower the next generation of researchers and clinicians in Latin America.

events.pageTitle: 3D Slicer for Latin America Outreach Events

soniaPujolIEEE2026.title: Dr. Sonia Pujol's IEEE EMBS Invited Talk, June 13, 2026, Montevideo, Uruguay
soniaPujolIEEE2026.description: Dr. Sonia Pujol delivered an invited keynote lecture at the IEEE EMBS Student Chapter, Universidad de la República (UdelaR), Montevideo, Uruguay, on June 13, 2026. Her presentation focused on 3D Slicer as a leading open-source platform for medical imaging and 3D digital modeling, as part of the Introduction to 3D Engineering in Medicine program. This marks the first official activity of the Slicer for Latin America project in Uruguay.

pw42.title: 42nd Slicer Project Week hackathon (PW42), Jan.27-31, 2025, Las Palmas, Gran Canaria, Spain
pw42.description: The 42nd Slicer Project Week was held in person at Las Palmas, Gran Canaria, Spain. Douglas Samuel Gonçalves, Lucas Sanchez Silva, and Victor Manuel Montaño Serrano attended in person. The team worked on the 3D Slicer for Latin America project, advancing Tutorial Maker with bug fixes, new features, automated testing infrastructure, and translations of tutorials to Portuguese and Spanish.

cziOpenScience2024.title: CZI Open Science 2024
cziOpenScience2024.description: Sonia Pujol presented the 3D Slicer for Latin America project at the Open Science in Latin America session of the 2024 CZI Open Science annual meeting.

cbeb2024.description: The Slicer for Latin America team presented a poster entitled "Preliminary Results of 3D Slicer Localization to Portuguese and Spanish for the Latin American Biomedical Engineering Community" at the XIX Brazilian Congress of Biomedical Engineering (CBEB2024), September 2-6, 2024, Ribeirão Preto, Brazil.

pw41.title: 41st Slicer Project Week hackathon (PW41), June 24-28, 2024, Cambridge, MA, USA
pw41.description: The 41st Slicer Project Week was held June 24–28, 2024, at MIT, Cambridge, MA. The Tutorial Maker module was showcased at the 3D Slicer for Latin America project and is being tested by the community.

workshopTolucaKidneysMay2024.description: The Slicer Toluca Workshop, held on May 7 & 14, 2024, in Toluca, Mexico, focused on Obtaining 3D models of the kidneys from CT imaging.

pw40.title: 40th Slicer Project Week hackathon (PW40), Jan.29 - Feb.2, 2024, Las Palmas, Gran Canaria, Spain
pw40.description: The 40th Slicer Project Week was held January 29 – February 2, 2024, at Las Palmas, Gran Canaria, Spain, and was the first in-person event for the Latin American team, who contributed to the 3D Slicer for Latin America project.

hackathonRP2023.title: Three-day 3D Slicer Hackathon, Oct.8-10, 2023, Ribeirão Preto, Brazil
hackathonRP2023.description: The Brazilian team organized a three-day 3D Slicer Hackathon within the XI Symposium on Medical Imaging and Instrumentation program held from October 8 to 10, 2023, at the University of São Paulo, Ribeirão Preto campus.

pw39.title: 39th Slicer Project Week hackathon (PW39), June 12-16, 2023, Montreal, Canada
pw39.description: The 39th Slicer Project Week was held June 12–16, 2023, at École de Technologie Supérieure, Montreal, Canada. The team participated with the 3D Slicer for Latin America project, advancing Tutorial Maker development and Slicer localization.

pw38.title: 38th Slicer Project Week hackathon (PW38), Jan.30 - Feb.3, 2023, Las Palmas, Gran Canaria, Spain
pw38.description: The 38th Slicer Project Week was held January 30 - February 3, 2023, in Las Palmas, Gran Canaria, Spain. Dr. Sonia Pujol hosted daily online sessions on 3D Slicer internationalization and introduced the "3D Slicer for Latin America: Localization and Outreach" project to the 3D Slicer community at the event. Dr. Adriana Vilchis González and Dr. Luiz Murta participated in the daily sessions that brought together 23 participants from Mexico, Brazil, Senegal, Hungary, Mauritania, the USA, and Canada. Dr. Pujol gave a training tutorial on how to use 3D Slicer internationalization infrastructure to translate 3D Slicer in multiple languages at the event. The Latin American team members contributed to the 3D Slicer Internationalization project.

rsna2022.title: 108th Annual Meeting of the Radiological Society of North America (RSNA 2022), Nov.27 - Dec.1, 2022, Chicago, IL, USA
rsna2022.description: We introduced the "3D Slicer for Latin America: Localization and Outreach" project in our educational exhibit entitled "The 3D Slicer open source software platform for medical image processing, 3D visualization and image-guided therapy" (Sonia Pujol, PhD, Steve Pieper, PhD, Andras Lasso, PhD, Andriy Fedorov, PhD, Ron Kikinis, MD) at the 108th Annual Meeting of the Radiological Society of North America (RSNA 2022).

claib2022.title: IX Latin American Congress on Biomedical Engineering (CLAIB 2022) and the XXVIII Brazilian Congress on Engineering Biomedical Engineering (CBEB 2022), Oct.24-28, 2022, Santa Catarina, Brazil
claib2022.description: The project was presented at the CLAIB 2022 / CBEB 2022 conference, held October 24–28, 2022, in Santa Catarina, Brazil.

description.title: Description
description.p1: The Tutorial Maker is a tool to automate the creation of tutorials within the 3D Slicer environment. To achieve this goal, a project management methodology has been implemented, combining weekly virtual meetings and using the GitHub platform as a code development and collaboration system version control.
description.p2: This tool was developed by The 3D Slicer Latin America team, whose members have made significant progress in developing the Tutorial Maker Module, focusing on improving its functionality, usability, and accessibility.

objectives.title: Objectives
objectives.intro: Among the objectives of the tutorial maker tool are the following:
objectives.internationalizationDesc: The strings of the module's user interface were marked, enabling easy translation into multiple languages. The module's installation steps in Spanish, Portuguese, and English are documented.
objectives.accessibilityDesc: A simplified installation and use of the module allows a broader range of users to benefit from the tool.
objectives.flexibilityDesc: The possibility to generate tutorials in multiple formats broadens the possibilities for distributing and utilizing the created content.
objectives.userExperienceDesc: Interface optimizations and new features make the process of creating tutorials more intuitive and efficient.
objectives.sustainabilityDesc: Process automation and improved project management ensure the module's continuous development and long-term maintenance.

features.title: Features
features.automatedDesc: Streamlines the creation of 3D Slicer tutorials by automatically running workflows and capturing screenshots for each step, reducing manual effort.
features.annotationDesc: Includes a built-in editor to add arrows, text boxes, highlights, and other visual cues directly onto your tutorial screenshots to improve clarity.
features.exportDesc: Allows users to save annotated screenshots and generate content suitable for various documentation formats (HTML, PDF, Markdown).
features.multiLangDesc: Designed to facilitate the creation and localization of tutorials in multiple languages (including English, Spanish, Portuguese and French), making Slicer accessible to a wider audience.

repository.title: Repository
repository.description: The Tutorial Maker tool repository contains a step-by-step installation process and is constantly updated according to the improvements introduced by the team.

collection.title: Slicer Tutorial Maker Collection
collection.description: The Slicer Tutorial Maker Collection showcases tutorials created with the Tutorial Maker extension, covering topics from basic 3D Slicer navigation to AI-based segmentation.

localization.title: Localization
localization.description: The Localization page contains the translated tutorials (Portuguese and Spanish) and the original English versions.

localization.intro: As part of the 3D Slicer for Latin America project, 14 tutorials were manually localized to Latin American Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese. The tutorials are presented below, including the original English versions for reference.

glossary.title: Localized Glossary
glossary.intro: The following glossaries provide localized terminology for 3D Slicer in Latin American Spanish and Brazilian Portuguese.

tutorialCollection.intro: The Slicer Tutorial Maker Collection is a curated set of tutorials created with the Slicer Tutorial Maker extension. Each tutorial is automatically generated by running a Python script inside 3D Slicer, which captures annotated screenshots and produces outputs in HTML, PDF, and Markdown formats. The tutorials are available in multiple languages and serve as both learning resources and reference implementations for authors creating new tutorials.

welcome.title: Welcome Tutorial
welcome.li4: Ideal starting point for first-time users

fourMinute.li1: Quick overview of core Slicer features

sponsors.description: The '3D Slicer for Latin America' project has been made possible through an Essential Open Source Software for Science (EOSS cycle 5) grant from the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.
